description: Extensible library for opening URLs. Provides functions and classes for HTTP authentication, redirections, cookies, and more.

functions.name: urlopen

params.name: url
params.description: URL string or Request object to open

params.name: data
params.description: Additional data to send to the server. If provided, the request becomes a POST. Should be a buffer in standard application/x-www-form-urlencoded format.

params.name: timeout
params.description: Timeout in seconds for blocking operations like the connection attempt. If not specified, the global default timeout is used.

returns.description: A file-like object with read(), readline(), readlines(), fileno(), close(), info(), getcode(), and geturl() methods

functions.description: Open the URL, which can be either a string or a Request object. Returns a file-like response object. For HTTP and HTTPS URLs, returns an httplib.HTTPResponse-wrapped object.

functions.name: install_opener

params.name: opener
params.description: An OpenerDirector instance to install as the default global opener

functions.description: Install an OpenerDirector instance as the default global opener. Subsequent calls to urlopen() will use the installed opener.

functions.name: build_opener

params.name: *handlers
params.description: Handler instances or handler classes to use. Classes are instantiated without arguments. Several default handlers are always included unless overridden.

returns.description: An OpenerDirector instance with the given handlers and default handlers chained together

functions.description: Return an OpenerDirector instance which chains the given handlers in order. Default handlers include ProxyHandler, UnknownHandler, HTTPHandler, HTTPDefaultErrorHandler, HTTPRedirectHandler, FTPHandler, FileHandler, HTTPErrorProcessor. Handlers can be instances or classes.

classes.name: Request
classes.description: Abstraction of a URL request. Encapsulates a URL, headers, data, and request type.

params.name: url
params.description: The URL to request. Must be a valid URL string.

params.name: data
params.description: Data to send with the request. If provided, the request becomes a POST. Should be in application/x-www-form-urlencoded format or any bytes payload.

params.name: headers
params.description: Dictionary of HTTP headers. Each key is a header name, each value is the header value. Header names are case-insensitive.

params.name: origin_req_host
params.description: The host of the origin transaction, as defined by RFC 2965. Defaults to cookielib.request_host(self).

params.name: unverifiable
params.description: Whether the request is unverifiable as defined by RFC 2965. Defaults to False.

constructors.description: Create a new Request object for the given URL with optional data and headers.

methods.name: add_header

params.name: key
params.description: Header name

params.name: val
params.description: Header value

methods.description: Add a header to the request. Currently does not check if the header already exists; duplicate headers will be appended. The header is stored with the key capitalized.

methods.name: add_data

params.name: data
params.description: Data to set on the request

methods.description: Set the request data. This changes the request method from GET to POST.

methods.name: add_unredirected_header

params.name: key
params.description: Header name

params.name: val
params.description: Header value

methods.description: Add a header that will not be sent on redirect. Used for headers that should only be sent on the original request.

methods.name: has_header

params.name: header
params.description: Header name to check

returns.description: True if the header is present (either regular or unredirected)

methods.description: Return whether the request has the named header. Checks both regular and unredirected headers.

methods.name: get_header

params.name: header_name
params.description: Header name to retrieve

params.name: default
params.description: Default value if the header is not found

returns.description: Header value or default if not found

methods.description: Return the value of the given header. Checks regular headers first, then unredirected headers. Returns default if not found.

methods.name: header_items
methods.description: Return a list of tuples (header_name, header_value) of all headers in the request.

methods.name: get_full_url
methods.description: Return the full URL of the request.

methods.name: get_host

returns.description: The host portion of the URL

methods.description: Return the host to which the request will be sent.

methods.name: get_type
methods.description: Return the URL scheme (type) of the request.

methods.name: get_selector

returns.description: The path and query portion of the URL

methods.description: Return the selector (path + query) portion of the URL.

methods.name: set_proxy

params.name: host
params.description: Proxy host string

params.name: type
params.description: Proxy type (URL scheme)

methods.description: Prepare the request to connect via a proxy by setting the host and type to the proxy values.

methods.name: has_data

returns.description: True if the request has data

methods.description: Return whether the request has data (i.e. is a POST request).

methods.name: get_data
methods.description: Return the request data.

classes.name: OpenerDirector
classes.description: Manages a collection of handlers and coordinates opening URLs by dispatching to the appropriate handler chain.

constructors.description: Create a new OpenerDirector. Typically you should use build_opener() instead of instantiating directly.

methods.name: open

params.name: url
params.description: URL string or Request object to open

params.name: data
params.description: Data to send with the request

params.name: timeout
params.description: Timeout in seconds for the operation

methods.description: Open the given URL. Dispatches to installed handler chain. The url argument may be a string URL or a Request object.

methods.name: error

params.name: proto
params.description: Protocol scheme or 'default' for error handling

params.name: *args
params.description: Arguments to pass to the error handler

returns.description: Return value from the error handler

methods.description: Handle an error for the given protocol. Looks for a registered error handler and dispatches to it.

methods.name: add_handler

params.name: handler
params.description: Handler instance to add to the opener

methods.description: Add a handler to the opener. The handler is inserted into the chain and will be used for matching protocols and error handling.

classes.name: BaseHandler
classes.description: Base class for all URL handlers. Subclasses implement protocol-specific open and error methods. Handlers are registered with an OpenerDirector.

fields.name: parent
fields.description: The OpenerDirector this handler is registered with. Set by add_handler().

classes.name: HTTPHandler
classes.description: Handler for opening HTTP URLs. Uses httplib.HTTPConnection.

params.name: debuglevel
params.description: Debug level for httplib. Set to 1 to print HTTP request/response details.

constructors.description: Create an HTTP handler with the given debug level.

classes.name: HTTPSHandler
classes.description: Handler for opening HTTPS URLs. Uses httplib.HTTPSConnection. Requires ssl module support.

params.name: debuglevel
params.description: Debug level for httplib. Set to 1 to print HTTPS request/response details.

params.name: context
params.description: SSL context for configuring SSL options. If not provided, a default context is used.

constructors.description: Create an HTTPS handler with the given debug level and optional SSL context.

classes.name: ProxyHandler
classes.description: Handler for routing requests through proxy servers. Reads proxy settings from environment variables if no proxies dict is given.

params.name: proxies
params.description: Dictionary mapping protocol names to proxy URLs (e.g. {'http': 'http://proxy:8080'}). If None, reads from environment variables (http_proxy, https_proxy, etc.).

constructors.description: Create a proxy handler. If proxies is None, proxy settings are detected from environment variables.

classes.name: HTTPBasicAuthHandler
classes.description: Handler for HTTP Basic Authentication. Retries requests with credentials when a 401 response is received.

params.name: password_mgr
params.description: Password manager providing credentials. If None, an HTTPPasswordMgr is created.

constructors.description: Create a Basic Auth handler with the given password manager.

classes.name: HTTPPasswordMgr
classes.description: Manages a mapping of (realm, URI) to (user, password) pairs. Used by authentication handlers to look up credentials.

methods.name: add_password

params.name: realm
params.description: Authentication realm. Use None to match any realm.

params.name: uri
params.description: URI or sequence of URIs that these credentials apply to

params.name: user
params.description: Username

params.name: passwd
params.description: Password

methods.description: Add credentials for the given realm and URI(s).

methods.name: find_user_password

params.name: realm
params.description: Authentication realm to look up

params.name: authuri
params.description: URI to look up credentials for

returns.description: Tuple of (user, password) or (None, None) if no matching credentials found

methods.description: Find credentials for the given realm and URI. Searches registered URIs from longest to shortest path.

classes.name: HTTPPasswordMgrWithDefaultRealm
classes.description: A password manager that also supports a default realm. Credentials added with realm=None will match any realm for the given URI.

constructors.description: Create a new password manager with default realm support.

methods.name: add_password

params.name: realm
params.description: Authentication realm. Use None as the default realm that matches any challenge.

params.name: uri
params.description: URI or sequence of URIs that these credentials apply to

params.name: user
params.description: Username

params.name: passwd
params.description: Password

methods.description: Add credentials for the given realm and URI(s). If realm is None, the credentials serve as a fallback for any realm.

methods.name: find_user_password

params.name: realm
params.description: Authentication realm to look up

params.name: authuri
params.description: URI to look up credentials for

returns.description: Tuple of (user, password) or (None, None) if no matching credentials found

methods.description: Find credentials for the given realm and URI. If no match for the specified realm, falls back to credentials stored under None realm.

classes.name: HTTPCookieProcessor
classes.description: Handler for automatic cookie handling. Stores cookies from responses and sends them with subsequent requests.

params.name: cookiejar
params.description: CookieJar instance to use for storing cookies. If None, a cookielib.CookieJar is created.

constructors.description: Create a cookie handler. If cookiejar is None, a new CookieJar is created.

fields.name: cookiejar
fields.description: The CookieJar instance used to store and retrieve cookies

classes.name: URLError
classes.description: Exception raised when a URL cannot be opened. Subclass of IOError.

fields.name: reason
fields.description: The reason for the error. A string or an underlying socket exception.

classes.name: HTTPError
classes.description: Exception raised for HTTP errors (non-success status codes). Subclass of URLError and also acts as a file-like response object, so it can be read directly.

params.name: url
params.description: The URL that caused the error

params.name: code
params.description: HTTP status code

params.name: msg
params.description: HTTP reason phrase

params.name: hdrs
params.description: Response headers

params.name: fp
params.description: File-like body of the response

constructors.description: Create an HTTPError. Usually raised by handlers, not instantiated directly.

methods.name: read
methods.description: Read the response body. HTTPError doubles as a file-like response object.

fields.name: msg
fields.description: HTTP reason phrase (e.g. 'Not Found', 'Internal Server Error')

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL that caused the error

fields.name: reason
fields.description: Alias for msg. Inherited from URLError.
